configuration.material: Polyurethane main body, Aramid core wire (HTUN type)

confirmed: Belt type S3M (3 mm pitch) -- manufacturer-confirmed (configured product page + family spec table)
Width 10 mm (Misumi '100' width code) -- manufacturer-confirmed; also CAD-measured (Z-extent = 10.0 mm exactly)
Closed loop / endless -- manufacturer-confirmed ('Belt Type: Closed End' on configured page; family page explicitly distinguishes Closed End vs Open End)
Real purchasable catalog PN with published pitch length (540 mm) and tooth count (180) -- manufacturer-confirmed (Configured Specifications block, price $16.72, ship date populated)
Brand = MISUMI (MSM1) -- confirmed; CAD retrieved hands-off with no 403/504 (cadFormatList returned 15 formats, cadDownload succeeded on first poll)
Pitch length within 540-580 mm target band -- 540 mm is the closest published standard length at the low end of the band; teeth=180 within the ~180-195 estimate
unresolved: Exact required center-distance for the real idler pair (~250-290 mm) was not given as a hard number to re-derive the optimal pitch length against; 540 mm (180T) assumed acceptable per the job packet's own target band. If the lead's actual idler center-distance computation lands closer to 564 mm (188T), the alternative HTUN564S3M-100 is already confirmed available at the identical price/lead-time -- swap is a non-issue.

evidence.drawing: candidate_evidence/rung2-s3m-belt/source/HTUN540S3M-100_drawing.png (generic S3M tooth-profile dimension drawing -- shared family drawing, does not depict circumference)
evidence.specification: candidate_evidence/rung2-s3m-belt/source/HTUN540S3M-100_page.txt and family_page_full.txt (full PN-grammar + length/teeth table for the whole S3M Closed-End High-Torque family)
evidence.bom: not applicable -- single discrete catalog item, no BOM structure

cad_measurements.feature: tooth pitch / profile (pitch 3mm, H 1.94mm, h 1.14mm, PLD 0.381mm)
cad_measurements.basis: manufacturer-confirmed (spec table) -- NOT visible in CAD; CAD body is a simplified non-toothed placeholder strip
cad_measurements.agrees_with_manufacturer_spec: not applicable -- CAD does not model this feature

cad_measurements.basis: manufacturer-confirmed (configured product page) -- explicitly NOT CAD-derivable; the CAD bounding box (X-extent ~263 mm) does not correspond to circumference and must not be used to infer it

alternatives.evidence_depth: spec/configured page only (resolved cleanly); CAD not pulled -- reserve CAD-pull for this only if the lead's exact center-distance calc prefers 564 mm over 540 mm
alternatives.note: Identical price and lead time to the primary candidate. A second scout's tab (independently, outside this job) had already navigated to this exact PN, corroborating it as a known-good resolvable candidate.

alternatives.part_number: HTBN540S3M-100 (rubber variant, not directly resolved in this session)
alternatives.note: Same family, Rubber/Chloroprene + fiberglass-core type instead of Polyurethane/Aramid. Listed in the family table as available at 540 S3M / width 100. Not pulled -- polyurethane (HTUN) was prioritized since the job packet implies a light passive idler loop where back-side tensioning (HTUN's one limitation) is not a driving concern.

risks: Misumi's CAD for this belt family is a non-representative simplified placeholder body (rounded-end extruded strip, no teeth, no loop topology, non-watertight, ~4399 mm^3). Do NOT use it for tooth-mesh engagement geometry against the SHTF20S3M100-5 idlers or the SL-TBLG clamp jaw -- those mating checks must rely on the spec-table tooth dimensions (pitch 3, H 1.94, h 1.14, PLD 0.381) common to the whole S3M series, already used elsewhere in the project for the clamp-jaw confirmation.

If final belt-loop geometry generation (the one allowed generated element per CLAUDE.md) needs an actual length to route, 540 mm pitch length / 180 teeth is the authoritative manufacturer number to use -- not anything read off this CAD mesh.

Polyurethane (HTUN) belts 'cannot be used with tension from back side' per the family caution note -- if the rung2 design tensions or backs the idler against the belt's outer (non-toothed) face under load, the rubber HTBN540S3M-100 variant (also confirmed available at the same length/width) would be the safer pick. Flagging for lead decision; not investigated further here.
recommended_next_action: Lead to confirm 540 mm / 180-tooth pitch length is compatible with the actual computed idler center-distance (HTUN564S3M-100 / 188T is a ready fallback at identical price/lead-time if not). If belt back-tensioning is part of the load path, consider swapping to HTBN540S3M-100 (rubber) instead of HTUN. Once selected, the lead performs canonical ontology authoring -- this bundle only supplies evidence, no ports/attachments have been created.
